title: The Tear.
content:
On beds of snow the moonbeam slept,
        And chilly was the midnight gloom,
    When by the damp grave Ellen wept--
        Fond maid! it was her Lindor's tomb!

    A warm tear gushed, the wintry air,
        Congealed it as it flowed away:
    All night it lay an ice-drop there,
        At morn it glittered in the ray.

    An angel, wandering from her sphere,
        Who saw this bright, this frozen gem,
    To dew-eyed Pity brought the tear
        And hung it on her diadem!
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Tear."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
